records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3818 - Aban narrated from Ismail ibn Al-Fadl, who said: "I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about purchasing a slave from the People of the Book (Ahl al-Dhimma)."
Imam (as) replied: "If they acknowledge ownership of the slave, then buy and marry."
records.arabicText: 3818 - وَ رَوَى أَبَانٌ عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ اَلْفَضْلِ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ شِرَاءِ مَمْلُوكِ أَهْلِ اَلذِّمَّةِ فَقَالَ "إِذَا أَقَرُّوا لَهُمْ بِذَلِكَ فَاشْتَرِ وَ اِنْكِحْ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3818 - Aban narrated from Ismail ibn Al-Fadl, who said: "I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about purchasing a slave from the People of the Book (Ahl al-Dhimma)."
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If they acknowledge ownership of the slave, then buy and marry."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3819 - Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Abdillah narrated from Abu Abdillah (as). He said: "I asked Imam (as) about a man who buys a bondwoman, then has relations with her, and later discovers that she is pregnant."
Imam (as) replied: "He must return her and also return something along with her."
records.arabicText: 3819 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَشْتَرِي اَلْجَارِيَةَ فَيَقَعُ عَلَيْهَا فَيَجِدُهَا حُبْلَى فَقَالَ "يَرُدُّهَا وَ يَرُدُّ مَعَهَا شَيْئاً".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3819 - Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Abdillah narrated from Abu Abdillah (as). He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I asked Imam (as) about a man who buys a bondwoman, then has relations with her, and later discovers that she is pregnant."
Imam (as) replied: "He must return her and also return something along with her."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3820 - In the narration of Abdul-Malik ibn Amr from Abu Abdillah (as):
"He must return her and also return half a tenth of her price if she is pregnant."
records.arabicText: 3820 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ عَبْدِ اَلْمَلِكِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "يَرُدُّهَا وَ يَرُدُّ نِصْفَ عُشْرِ ثَمَنِهَا إِذَا كَانَتْ حُبْلَى".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3820 - In the narration of Abdul-Malik ibn Amr from Abu Abdillah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He must return her and also return half a tenth of her price if she is pregnant."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3821 - In the narration of Muhammad ibn Muslim from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as): "He must return her and provide her with clothing."
records.arabicText: 3821 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "يَرُدُّهَا وَ يَكْسُوهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3821 - In the narration of Muhammad ibn Muslim from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He must return her and provide her with clothing."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3822 - Muhammad ibn Maythar narrates from Abu Abdullah (as):
"Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) would not return a bondwoman due to a defect if she had been intimate, but he would claim compensation for the value of the defect.
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) used to say, 'I seek refuge in Allah (swt) from making her a source of payment.'"
records.arabicText: 3822 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُيَسِّرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "كَانَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ لاَ يَرُدُّ اَلْجَارِيَةَ بِعَيْبٍ إِذَا وُطِئَتْ وَ لَكِنْ يَرْجِعُ بِقِيمَةِ اَلْعَيْبِ وَ كَانَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ "مَعَاذَ اَللَّهِ أَنْ أَجْعَلَ لَهَا أَجْراً" ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3822 - Muhammad ibn Maythar narrates from Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) would not return a bondwoman due to a defect if she had been intimate, but he would claim compensation for the value of the defect.
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) used to say, 'I seek refuge in Allah (swt) from making her a source of payment.'"

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3823 - Ishaq ibn Ammar narrates: I said to Abu Ibrahim (Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as)): A man directs another man to a commodity and says, 'Buy it, and half of it will be mine.' The man buys it and pays from his own money.
The Imam (as) replied: "He is entitled to half the profit."
I asked: "What if there is a loss; does he also bear part of the loss?"
The Imam (as) said: "Yes, he is liable for the loss just as he shares in the profit."
records.arabicText: 3823 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ رَجُلٌ يَدُلُّ اَلرَّجُلَ عَلَى اَلسِّلْعَةِ وَ يَقُولُ اِشْتَرِهَا وَ لِيَ نِصْفُهَا فَيَشْتَرِيهَا اَلرَّجُلُ وَ يَنْقُدُ مِنْ مَالِهِ قَالَ لَهُ نِصْفُ اَلرِّبْحِ قُلْتُ فَإِنْ وُضِعَ لَحِقَهُ مِنَ اَلْوَضِيعَةِ شَيْءٌ فَقَالَ "نَعَمْ عَلَيْهِ اَلْوَضِيعَةُ كَمَا يَأْخُذُ اَلرِّبْحَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3823 - Ishaq ibn Ammar narrates: I said to Abu Ibrahim (Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as)): A man directs another man to a commodity and says, 'Buy it, and half of it will be mine.' The man buys it and pays from his own money.
The Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"He is entitled to half the profit."
I asked: "What if there is a loss; does he also bear part of the loss?"
The Imam (as) said: "Yes, he is liable for the loss just as he shares in the profit."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3824 - Hamzah ibn Humran narrates: I said to Abu Abdullah (as): I enter the market intending to buy a bondwoman, but she claims that she is free.
The Imam (as) replied: "Buy her unless she has evidence (to prove her claim of freedom)."
records.arabicText: 3824 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ حَمْزَةَ بْنِ حُمْرَانَ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَدْخُلُ اَلسُّوقَ أُرِيدُ أَنْ أَشْتَرِيَ جَارِيَةً فَتَقُولُ إِنِّي حُرَّةٌ قَالَ "اِشْتَرِهَا إِلاَّ أَنْ تَكُونَ لَهَا بَيِّنَةٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3824 - Hamzah ibn Humran narrates: I said to Abu Abdullah (as): I enter the market intending to buy a bondwoman, but she claims that she is free.
The Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Buy her unless she has evidence (to prove her claim of freedom)."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3825 - Al-Is ibn al-Qasim asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a slave who claimed that he was free but did not provide any evidence for his claim.
The Imam (as) replied: "Yes, you may buy him."
records.arabicText: 3825 - وَ سَأَلَهُ اَلْعِيصُ بْنُ اَلْقَاسِمِ : عَنْ مَمْلُوكٍ اِدَّعَى أَنَّهُ حُرٌّ وَ لَمْ يَأْتِ بِبَيِّنَةٍ عَلَى ذَلِكَ أَشْتَرِيهِ قَالَ "نَعَمْ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3825 - Al-Is ibn al-Qasim asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a slave who claimed that he was free but did not provide any evidence for his claim.
The Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Yes, you may buy him."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3826 - Muhammad ibn Qays narrated from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
"Commander of the Faithful (as) ruled concerning a female slave who was sold by the son of her master while her master was absent. The buyer took her as a concubine, and she bore him a son. Later, her original master returned and disputed with the buyer, claiming: 'She is my slave, and my son sold her without my permission.'
The Imam (as) said: 'The ruling is that the master may reclaim both his female slave and her son.'
The buyer pleaded with him, saying: 'Take your son who sold her to me, but I will not release your son until you release mine.'
When the master of the female slave saw this, he accepted and approved the sale conducted by his son."
records.arabicText: 3826 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ قَيْسٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "قَضَى أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فِي وَلِيدَةٍ بَاعَهَا اِبْنُ سَيِّدِهَا وَ أَبُوهُ غَائِبٌ فَتَسَرَّاهَا اَلَّذِي اِشْتَرَاهَا فَوَلَدَتْ مِنْهُ غُلاَماً ثُمَّ جَاءَ سَيِّدُهَا اَلْأَوَّلُ يُخَاصِمُ سَيِّدَهَا اَلْآخَرَ فَقَالَ وَلِيدَتِي بَاعَهَا اِبْنِي بِغَيْرِ إِذْنِي قَالَ "اَلْحُكْمُ أَنْ يَأْخُذَ وَلِيدَتَهُ وَ اِبْنَهَا" فَيُنَاشِدَهُ اَلَّذِي اِشْتَرَاهَا فَقَالَ لَهُ "خُذِ اِبْنَهُ اَلَّذِي بَاعَكَ وَ تَقُولُ لاَ وَ اَللَّهِ لاَ أُرْسِلُ اِبْنَكَ حَتَّى تُرْسِلَ اِبْنِي" فَلَمَّا رَأَى ذَلِكَ سَيِّدُ اَلْوَلِيدَةِ أَجَازَ بَيْعَ اِبْنِهِ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3826 - Muhammad ibn Qays narrated from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
"Commander of the Faithful (as) ruled concerning a female slave who was sold by the son of her master while her master was absent. The buyer took her as a concubine, and she bore him a son. Later, her original master returned and disputed with the buyer, claiming: 'She is my slave, and my son sold her without my permission.'
The Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
'The ruling is that the master may reclaim both his female slave and her son.'
The buyer pleaded with him, saying: 'Take your son who sold her to me, but I will not release your son until you release mine.'
When the master of the female slave saw this, he accepted and approved the sale conducted by his son."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3827 - Ibn Sinan narrated that Abu Abdullah (as) said:
"Regarding a man who buys a male or female slave, and the slave has a brother, sister, father, or mother in one of the cities, he should not take the slave out of that city to another city if the slave is young, nor should he buy him.
However, if the slave has a mother and both she and the slave are content with the sale, then he may buy the slave if he wishes."
records.arabicText: 3827 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنِ اِبْنِ سِنَانٍ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلرَّجُلِ يَشْتَرِي اَلْغُلاَمَ أَوِ اَلْجَارِيَةَ وَ لَهُ أَخٌ أَوْ أُخْتٌ أَوْ أَبٌ أَوْ أُمٌّ بِمِصْرٍ مِنَ اَلْأَمْصَارِ قَالَ "لاَ يُخْرِجُهُ مِنْ مِصْرٍ إِلَى مِصْرٍ آخَرَ إِنْ كَانَ صَغِيراً وَ لاَ يَشْتَرِيهِ فَإِنْ كَانَتْ لَهُ أُمٌّ فَطَابَتْ نَفْسُهَا وَ نَفْسُهُ فَاشْتَرِهِ إِنْ شِئْتَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3827 - Ibn Sinan narrated that Abu Abdullah (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Regarding a man who buys a male or female slave, and the slave has a brother, sister, father, or mother in one of the cities, he should not take the slave out of that city to another city if the slave is young, nor should he buy him.
However, if the slave has a mother and both she and the slave are content with the sale, then he may buy the slave if he wishes."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3828 - Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi, from Abu Abdullah (as):
He was asked about walnuts that cannot be counted individually, so they are measured with a container and then counted based on the amount in that container. Afterward, the remaining quantity is measured according to that count.
Imam (as) said: "There is no problem with that."
records.arabicText: 3828 - وَ رَوَى حَمَّادٌ عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنِ اَلْجَوْزِ لاَ نَسْتَطِيعُ أَنْ نَعُدَّهُ فَيُكَالُ بِمِكْيَالٍ ثُمَّ يُعَدُّ مَا فِيهِ ثُمَّ يُكَالُ مَا بَقِيَ عَلَى حِسَابِ ذَلِكَ مِنَ اَلْعَدَدِ قَالَ "لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3828 - Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi, from Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
He was asked about walnuts that cannot be counted individually, so they are measured with a container and then counted based on the amount in that container. Afterward, the remaining quantity is measured according to that count.
Imam (as) said: "There is no problem with that."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3829 - Al-Halabi narrated from Abu Abdullah (as):
"Any food for which a specific measure has been stated cannot be sold by estimation. This is among the practices that are disliked in selling food."
records.arabicText: 3829 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "مَا كَانَ مِنْ طَعَامٍ سَمَّيْتَ فِيهِ كَيْلاً فَلاَ يَصْلُحُ بَيْعُهُ مُجَازَفَةً هَذَا مِمَّا يُكْرَهُ مِنْ بَيْعِ اَلطَّعَامِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3829 - Al-Halabi narrated from Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Any food for which a specific measure has been stated cannot be sold by estimation. This is among the practices that are disliked in selling food."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3830 - Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj narrated from Abu Abdullah (as):
"I asked Imam (as) about a man who purchases a commodity with a dirham, and the dirham is deficient by a fraction, such as a grain or the like. Should he give it to the seller without informing him that it is deficient?"
Imam (as) said: 'No, unless it is something like these small coins (al-wadaḥiyyah), which are commonly accepted, just as we accept them by count.'"
records.arabicText: 3830 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ اَلْحَجَّاجِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَشْتَرِي اَلْمَبِيعَ بِالدِّرْهَمِ وَ هُوَ يَنْقُصُ اَلْحَبَّةَ وَ نَحْوَ ذَلِكَ أَ يُعْطِيهِ اَلَّذِي يَشْتَرِي مِنْهُ وَ لاَ يُعْلِمُهُ أَنَّهُ يَنْقُصُ قَالَ "لاَ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَكُونَ مِثْلَ هَذِهِ اَلْوَضَاحِيَّةِ يَجُوزُ، كَمَا يَجُوزُ عِنْدَنَا عَدَداً".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3830 - Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj narrated from Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I asked Imam (as) about a man who purchases a commodity with a dirham, and the dirham is deficient by a fraction, such as a grain or the like. Should he give it to the seller without informing him that it is deficient?"
Imam (as) said: 'No, unless it is something like these small coins (al-wadaḥiyyah), which are commonly accepted, just as we accept them by count.'"

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3831 - Samaah asked him about purchasing milk while it is still in the udders.
Imam (as) said: "No, unless he milks some of it into a small vessel (sukurjah) for you, and then you say, 'I purchase from you this milk that is in the vessel and what is in its udders for a specified price.' If there is nothing in the udders, then the sale applies to what is in the vessel."
records.arabicText: 3831 - وَ سَأَلَهُ سَمَاعَةُ : عَنِ اَللَّبَنِ يُشْتَرَى وَ هُوَ فِي اَلضُّرُوعِ فَقَالَ "لاَ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَحْلُبَ لَكَ مِنْهُ سُكُرُّجَةً فَتَقُولُ أَشْتَرِي مِنْكَ هَذَا اَللَّبَنَ اَلَّذِي فِي اَلسُّكُرُّجَةِ وَ مَا فِي ضُرُوعِهَا بِثَمَنٍ مُسَمًّى فَإِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ فِي اَلضُّرُوعِ شَيْءٌ كَانَ فِيمَا فِي اَلسُّكُرُّجَةِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3831 - Samaah asked him about purchasing milk while it is still in the udders.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "No, unless he milks some of it into a small vessel (sukurjah) for you, and then you say, 'I purchase from you this milk that is in the vessel and what is in its udders for a specified price.' If there is nothing in the udders, then the sale applies to what is in the vessel."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3832 - Aban narrated from Ismail ibn al-Fadl, who reported from Abu Abdillah (as):
I asked him about a man who accepts responsibility for the payment of poll tax (jizyah) for individuals, the land tax (kharaj) for palm trees, crops, forests, hunting grounds, fish, and birds, while he does not know whether any of these will produce revenue or not. Can he purchase and accept responsibility for it, and at what time should he make such a purchase and agreement?
Imam (as) said: "If you know that at least one of these sources will yield income, then you may purchase and accept responsibility for it."
records.arabicText: 3832 - وَ رَوَى أَبَانٌ عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ اَلْفَضْلِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَتَقَبَّلُ خَرَاجَ اَلرِّجَالِ وَ جِزْيَةَ رُءُوسِهِمْ وَ خَرَاجَ اَلنَّخْلِ وَ اَلشَّجَرِ وَ اَلْآجَامِ وَ اَلْمَصَايِدِ وَ اَلسَّمَكِ وَ اَلطَّيْرِ وَ هُوَ لاَ يَدْرِي لَعَلَّ هَذَا لاَ يَكُونُ أَبَداً أَوْ يَكُونُ أَ يَشْتَرِيهِ وَ فِي أَيِّ زَمَانٍ يَشْتَرِيهِ وَ يَتَقَبَّلُ مِنْهُ فَقَالَ "إِذَا عَلِمْتَ أَنَّ مِنْ ذَلِكَ شَيْئاً وَاحِداً قَدْ أَدْرَكَ فَاشْتَرِهِ وَ تَقَبَّلْ بِهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3832 - Aban narrated from Ismail ibn al-Fadl, who reported from Abu Abdillah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked him about a man who accepts responsibility for the payment of poll tax (jizyah) for individuals, the land tax (kharaj) for palm trees, crops, forests, hunting grounds, fish, and birds, while he does not know whether any of these will produce revenue or not. Can he purchase and accept responsibility for it, and at what time should he make such a purchase and agreement?
Imam (as) said: "If you know that at least one of these sources will yield income, then you may purchase and accept responsibility for it."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3833 - Zur'ah narrated from Sama'ah, from Abu Abdillah (as) regarding a man who purchases a slave while the slave has run away from his owner.
Imam (as) said: "It is not permissible for him unless he purchases something else along with him and says, 'I purchase from you this item and your slave for such and such amount.' If he is unable to obtain the slave, then the payment he made will apply to what he has purchased from him."
records.arabicText: 3833 - وَ رَوَى زُرْعَةُ عَنْ سَمَاعَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلرَّجُلِ يَشْتَرِي اَلْعَبْدَ وَ هُوَ آبِقٌ عَنْ أَهْلِهِ قَالَ "لاَ يَصْلُحُ لَهُ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَشْتَرِيَ مَعَهُ شَيْئاً آخَرَ وَ يَقُولَ أَشْتَرِي مِنْكَ هَذَا اَلشَّيْءَ وَ عَبْدَكَ بِكَذَا وَ كَذَا فَإِنْ لَمْ يَقْدِرْ عَلَى اَلْعَبْدِ كَانَ اَلثَّمَنُ اَلَّذِي نَقَدَهُ فِيمَا اِشْتَرَى مِنْهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3833 - Zur'ah narrated from Sama'ah, from Abu Abdillah (as) regarding a man who purchases a slave while the slave has run away from his owner.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "It is not permissible for him unless he purchases something else along with him and says, 'I purchase from you this item and your slave for such and such amount.' If he is unable to obtain the slave, then the payment he made will apply to what he has purchased from him."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3834 - Yaqub ibn Shuayb narrated:
I asked Abu Abdillah (as) about a man who owes me loads of goods measured by a specific scale, but he sends me loads that are less than the specified measurement I am owed. Can I take them without measuring precisely (mujazafah)?
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."
He said: I also asked Imam (as) about a man who owes another person a hundred kur of dates and owns date palms. He comes to him and says, "Give me these date palms in place of what you owe me."
It was as if Imam (as) disliked it.
He said: I asked Imam (as) about two men who jointly own date palms, and one of them says to the other, "Choose, either you take this date palm for such and such a specified measure and give me half of this measure, whether it increases or decreases, or I will take it under the same terms."
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."
records.arabicText:
3834 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ يَعْقُوبَ بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ قَالَ :
سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَكُونُ لِي عَلَيْهِ أَحْمَالٌ بِكَيْلٍ مُسَمًّى فَبَعَثَ إِلَيَّ بِأَحْمَالٍ مِنْهَا أَقَلُّ مِنَ اَلْكَيْلِ اَلَّذِي لِي عَلَيْهِ فَآخُذُهَا مُجَازَفَةً فَقَالَ "لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ" قَالَ وَ سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَكُونُ لَهُ عَلَى اَلْآخَرِ مِائَةُ كُرٍّ تَمْراً وَ لَهُ نَخْلٌ فَيَأْتِيهِ فَيَقُولُ أَعْطِنِي نَخْلَكَ هَذَا بِمَا عَلَيْكَ فَكَأَنَّهُ كَرِهَهُ قَالَ وَ سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلَيْنِ يَكُونُ بَيْنَهُمَا اَلنَّخْلُ فَيَقُولُ أَحَدُهُمَا لِصَاحِبِهِ اِخْتَرْ إِمَّا أَنْ تَأْخُذَ هَذَا اَلنَّخْلَ بِكَذَا وَ كَذَا كَيْلاً مُسَمًّى وَ تُعْطِيَنِي نِصْفَ هَذَا اَلْكَيْلِ زَادَ أَوْ نَقَصَ وَ إِمَّا أَنْ آخُذَهُ أَنَا بِذَلِكَ قَالَ "لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3834 - Yaqub ibn Shuayb narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Abu Abdillah (as) about a man who owes me loads of goods measured by a specific scale, but he sends me loads that are less than the specified measurement I am owed. Can I take them without measuring precisely (mujazafah)?
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."
He said: I also asked Imam (as) about a man who owes another person a hundred kur of dates and owns date palms. He comes to him and says, "Give me these date palms in place of what you owe me."
It was as if Imam (as) disliked it.
He said: I asked Imam (as) about two men who jointly own date palms, and one of them says to the other, "Choose, either you take this date palm for such and such a specified measure and give me half of this measure, whether it increases or decreases, or I will take it under the same terms."
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3835 - Jameel narrated from Zurara who said: I asked Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about a man who purchased straw from a threshing floor before it was threshed, each load of straw for a specified amount, and he took the straw and sold it before the grain was measured.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."
records.arabicText:
3835 - وَ رَوَى جَمِيلٌ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ قَالَ :
سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ اِشْتَرَى تِبْنَ بَيْدَرٍ قَبْلَ أَنْ يُدَاسَ تِبْنَ كُلِّ كُرٍّ بِشَيْءٍ مَعْلُومٍ فَيَأْخُذُ اَلتِّبْنَ وَ يَبِيعُهُ قَبْلَ أَنْ يُكَالَ اَلطَّعَامُ قَالَ "لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3835 - Jameel narrated from Zurara who said: I asked Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about a man who purchased straw from a threshing floor before it was threshed, each load of straw for a specified amount, and he took the straw and sold it before the grain was measured.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3836 - It is narrated from Abdul-Malik bin Amr who said:
I said to Abu Abdillah (as), "I purchase one hundred containers of oil, and I inspect one or two containers and weigh them, then take the rest based on that measurement."
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm."
records.arabicText: 3836 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَلْمَلِكِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَشْتَرِي مِائَةَ رَاوِيَةٍ مِنْ زَيْتٍ وَ أَعْتَرِضُ رَاوِيَةً أَوِ اِثْنَتَيْنِ وَ أَتَّزِنُهُمَا ثُمَّ آخُذُ سَائِرَهُ عَلَى قَدْرِ ذَلِكَ فَقَالَ "لاَ بَأْسَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3836 - It is narrated from Abdul-Malik bin Amr who said:
I said to Abu Abdillah (as), "I purchase one hundred containers of oil, and I inspect one or two containers and weigh them, then take the rest based on that measurement."
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "There is no harm."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3837 - Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi, from Abu Abdillah (as), who said:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who has a debt and holds collateral along with it, can he purchase it?
Imam (as) said: "Yes."
records.arabicText: 3837 - وَ رَوَى حَمَّادٌ عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَكُونُ لَهُ اَلدَّيْنُ وَ مَعَهُ رَهْنٌ أَ يَشْتَرِيهِ قَالَ "نَعَمْ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3837 - Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi, from Abu Abdillah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who has a debt and holds collateral along with it, can he purchase it?
Imam (as) said: "Yes."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3838 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Al-Halabi who said that Abu Abdillah (as) said: "Any food for which a specific measure has been stipulated cannot be sold without measurement."
records.arabicText: 3838 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مُسْكَانَ عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "مَا كَانَ مِنْ طَعَامٍ سَمَّيْتَ فِيهِ كَيْلاً فَلاَ يَصْلُحُ مُجَازَفَةً".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3838 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Al-Halabi who said that Abu Abdillah (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Any food for which a specific measure has been stipulated cannot be sold without measurement."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3839 - It is narrated from Dawood bin Sirhan, from Abu Abdillah (as), who said:
I had two sacks of musk-one was moist, and the other was dry. I started with the moist one and sold it.
Then I took the dry one to sell, but I found that I was not offered the price it was worth, and they would not pay me more than the price of the moist musk.
So I asked Imam (as) about that, whether it was permissible for me to mix them.
Imam (as) said: "No, unless you inform them."
He said: "So I mixed them and then informed them."
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it if you inform them."
records.arabicText: 3839 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ دَاوُدَ بْنِ سِرْحَانَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : كَانَ مَعِي جِرَابَانِ مِنْ مِسْكٍ أَحَدُهُمَا رَطْبٌ وَ اَلْآخَرُ يَابِسٌ فَبَدَأْتُ بِالرَّطْبِ فَبِعْتُهُ ثُمَّ أَخَذْتُ اَلْيَابِسَ أَبِيعُهُ فَإِذَا أَنَا لاَ أُعْطَى بِالْيَابِسِ اَلثَّمَنَ اَلَّذِي يَسْوَى وَ لاَ يَزِيدُونِّي عَلَى ثَمَنِ اَلرَّطْبِ فَسَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ ذَلِكَ أَ يَصْلُحُ لِي أَنْ أُنَدِّيَهُ قَالَ "لاَ إِلاَّ أَنْ تُعْلِمَهُمْ" قَالَ فَنَدَّيْتُهُ ثُمَّ أَعْلَمْتُهُمْ قَالَ "لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ إِذَا أَعْلَمْتَهُمْ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3839 - It is narrated from Dawood bin Sirhan, from Abu Abdillah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I had two sacks of musk-one was moist, and the other was dry. I started with the moist one and sold it.
Then I took the dry one to sell, but I found that I was not offered the price it was worth, and they would not pay me more than the price of the moist musk.
So I asked Imam (as) about that, whether it was permissible for me to mix them.
Imam (as) said: "No, unless you inform them."
He said: "So I mixed them and then informed them."
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it if you inform them."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3840 - It is narrated from Abdullah bin Sinan who said: I asked Abu Abdillah (as) about a child born out of wedlock, can he be sold, purchased, and employed as a servant?
Imam (as) said: "Yes."
I said: "Can he be married?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, but do not seek her offspring."
records.arabicText: 3840 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ وَلَدِ اَلزِّنَا أَ يُبَاعُ وَ يُشْتَرَى وَ يُسْتَخْدَمُ قَالَ "نَعَمْ " قُلْتُ فَيُسْتَنْكَحُ قَالَ "نَعَمْ وَ لاَ تَطْلُبُ وَلَدَهَا ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3840 - It is narrated from Abdullah bin Sinan who said: I asked Abu Abdillah (as) about a child born out of wedlock, can he be sold, purchased, and employed as a servant?
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Yes."
I said: "Can he be married?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, but do not seek her offspring."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3841 - Sama'ah asked him about purchasing stolen or embezzled goods.
Imam (as) said: "If you know that it is so, then no, unless it is something you are buying from the authorities."
records.arabicText: 3841 - وَ سَأَلَهُ سَمَاعَةُ : عَنْ شِرَاءِ اَلْخِيَانَةِ وَ اَلسَّرِقَةِ قَالَ "إِذَا عَرَفْتَ أَنَّهُ كَذَلِكَ فَلاَ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَكُونَ شَيْئاً تَشْتَرِيهِ مِنَ اَلْعُمَّالِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3841 - Sama'ah asked him about purchasing stolen or embezzled goods.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If you know that it is so, then no, unless it is something you are buying from the authorities."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3842 - Muhammad bin Al-Fudayl narrated from Abu Al-Sabbah Al-Kinani who said:
I asked Abu Abdillah (as) about a partnership in which a man is given wealth and instructed to take it to a specific land, but he disobeys and takes it to another land, and the wealth is lost.
Imam (as) said: "He is liable. However, if it is safe and profits are made, then the profit is to be shared between them."
records.arabicText: 3842 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ اَلْفُضَيْلِ عَنْ أَبِي اَلصَّبَّاحِ اَلْكِنَانِيِّ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلْمُضَارَبَةِ يُعْطَى اَلرَّجُلُ اَلْمَالَ فَيَخْرُجُ بِهِ إِلَى أَرْضٍ وَ يُنْهَى أَنْ يَخْرُجَ بِهِ إِلَى أَرْضٍ غَيْرِهَا فَعَصَى وَ خَرَجَ إِلَى أَرْضٍ أُخْرَى فَعَطِبَ اَلْمَالُ فَقَالَ "هُوَ ضَامِنٌ وَ إِنْ سَلِمَ وَ رَبِحَ فَالرِّبْحُ بَيْنَهُمَا ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3842 - Muhammad bin Al-Fudayl narrated from Abu Al-Sabbah Al-Kinani who said:
I asked Abu Abdillah (as) about a partnership in which a man is given wealth and instructed to take it to a specific land, but he disobeys and takes it to another land, and the wealth is lost.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He is liable. However, if it is safe and profits are made, then the profit is to be shared between them."
